dependencies.relationship: PATHandler uses APIError() and apikit.TokenPrefix from server_core; Cache-Control middleware from server_core applies to all PAT routes
PATHandler uses db.DB, db.WithTx, db.WrapError, db.ErrNotFound, db.FormatTime, db.ParseTime from database_layer
PATHandler uses auth.GetAuthInfo(), auth.GetUserID(), auth.RequirePermission(), and auth.PermissionRegistry from auth_middleware

task_groups.title: Implement internal token generation and hashing utilities

subtasks.title: Define tokenAlphabet constant and PATHandler struct
details: Create internal/handlers/pat.go.
Define const tokenAlphabet = "abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789".
Define PATHandler struct with fields db *db.DB and registry *auth.PermissionRegistry.
Implement NewPATHandler(database *db.DB, registry *auth.PermissionRegistry) *PATHandler with nil guards that panic with descriptive messages.
Add stub RegisterRoutes(g *echo.Group) method body with panic("not implemented").

subtasks.title: Implement generateTokenID and generateSecret using crypto/rand
details: Implement generateTokenID() (string, error): use crypto/rand.Read to fill a byte slice, map each byte modulo len(tokenAlphabet) to a character, return 8-char result.
Implement generateSecret() (string, error): same approach, return 32-char result.
On crypto/rand.Read error, return empty string and non-nil error.
Ensure no unbounded retry loops; read exactly the needed bytes.

subtasks.title: Implement hashSecret using crypto/sha256 and encoding/hex
details: Implement hashSecret(secret string) string.
Use sha256.Sum256([]byte(secret)) to get [32]byte digest.
Use hex.EncodeToString(digest[:]) to convert to lowercase hex.
Function is pure and deterministic — no error return needed.

All existing tests still pass: go test ./... -count=1
No linter errors: go vet ./...

task_groups.title: Implement CreatePAT handler — validation and privilege escalation

subtasks.title: Implement CreatePAT request body parsing and name/permissions validation
details: Define CreatePATRequest struct with Name string, Permissions []string, Expires *int json tags.
In CreatePAT handler, decode JSON body via c.Bind(); on error return APIError(c, 400, 'invalid request body').
Validate name: empty → 400 'name is required'; len > 255 → 400 'name must be 255 characters or fewer'.
Validate permissions: nil or empty → 400 'permissions are required'.

For each permission: count colons; != 1 → 400 'invalid permission format:

'; not in registry → 400 'unknown permission:

'. Stop at first error.
Call auth.RequirePermission(c, 'tokens', 'manage') before validation (or after — per execution path, before body parsing).

subtasks.title: Implement expires validation and privilege escalation check
details: Validate expires: if nil, default to 90; if value not in {0, 30, 60, 90} → 400 'expires must be 0, 30, 60, or 90'.
After registry validation, call auth.GetAuthInfo(c) to determine credential type.

If credential is a PAT, iterate requested permissions; for each not in creating PAT's permissions → 403 'cannot grant permission:

'.
If credential is an API key, skip escalation check entirely.

All existing tests still pass: go test ./... -count=1
No linter errors: go vet ./...
09-REQ-3 and 09-REQ-4 acceptance criteria met

task_groups.title: Implement CreatePAT handler — token generation, storage, and response

subtasks.title: Implement token generation, hashing, and expires_at calculation
details: After validation, call generateTokenID(); on error → 500 'internal server error'.
Call generateSecret(); on error → 500 'internal server error'.
Compute secretHash = hashSecret(secret).
Construct token = fmt.Sprintf('%s_pat_%s_%s', apikit.TokenPrefix, tokenID, secret).
Capture createdAt = time.Now().UTC().
Calculate expiresAt: if expires == 0, expiresAt = nil; otherwise expiresAt = createdAt + expires*24*time.Hour.

subtasks.title: Implement transactional INSERT into pats table
details: Serialize permissions []string to JSON using json.Marshal; on error → 500.
Call db.WithTx(func(tx) error) wrapping the INSERT: token_id, user_id, name, secret_hash, permissions (JSON), expires_days, expires_at, created_at.
Use db.FormatTime for timestamp columns.
On WithTx error → 500 'internal server error' via APIError(); transaction auto-rolled-back.

subtasks.title: Construct and return CreatePATResponse with Cache-Control header
details: Define CreatePATResponse struct: TokenID, Name, Token, Permissions []string, ExpiresAt *string, CreatedAt string.
Populate response with token_id, name, plaintext token, permissions (insertion order), formatted expires_at (null if nil), created_at.
Omit revoked_at and expires_days from response struct (no field defined).
Set response header Cache-Control: no-store before returning.
Return c.JSON(201, resp).
Allow duplicate names without error (no uniqueness check in handler).

All existing tests still pass: go test ./... -count=1
No linter errors: go vet ./...

subtasks.title: Implement ListPATs query and response serialization
details: Implement ListPATs(c echo.Context) error handler.
Call auth.RequirePermission(c, 'tokens', 'read'); on fail return early (middleware handles response).
Get userID via auth.GetUserID(c).
Query pats table: SELECT token_id, name, permissions, created_at, expires_at, revoked_at FROM pats WHERE user_id = ? ORDER BY created_at DESC.
For each row, parse permissions JSON via json.Unmarshal; parse timestamps via db.ParseTime.
Build []PATResponse; if no rows, return empty slice (not nil) to produce JSON [].
Return c.JSON(200, results) with Cache-Control: no-store header.

subtasks.title: Handle ListPATs error cases and empty result
details: On DB query error → APIError(c, 500, 'internal server error').
On empty result set → return c.JSON(200, []PATResponse{}) — ensures JSON [] not null.
Verify PATResponse struct has no secret_hash, token, or expires_days JSON tags.
Auth middleware enforces tokens:read; handler does not duplicate that check beyond RequirePermission call.

subtasks.title: Implement GetPAT query with dual-column user isolation
details: Implement GetPAT(c echo.Context) error handler.
Call auth.RequirePermission(c, 'tokens', 'read').
Extract token_id from c.Param('token_id').
Get userID via auth.GetUserID(c).
Query: SELECT token_id, name, permissions, created_at, expires_at, revoked_at FROM pats WHERE token_id = ? AND user_id = ?.
If row not found (db.ErrNotFound or zero rows) → APIError(c, 404, 'token not found') — both nonexistent and other-user tokens return 404.
On other DB error → APIError(c, 500, 'internal server error').
Parse permissions JSON and timestamps; build PATResponse; return c.JSON(200, resp) with Cache-Control: no-store.

checks: GetPAT tests pass: go test ./internal/handlers/... -count=1 -run 'TestGetPAT|TestCreateAndGet'
All existing tests still pass: go test ./... -count=1

09-REQ-7 acceptance criteria met; TS-09-SMOKE-3 passes

subtasks.title: Implement conditional UPDATE and zero-row disambiguation logic
details: Implement RevokePAT(c echo.Context) error handler.
Call auth.RequirePermission(c, 'tokens', 'manage').
Extract token_id from c.Param('token_id'); get userID via auth.GetUserID(c).
Execute UPDATE pats SET revoked_at = ? WHERE token_id = ? AND user_id = ? AND revoked_at IS NULL.
If rowsAffected == 1: query the updated row; build PATResponse with revoked_at; return c.JSON(200, resp).
If rowsAffected == 0: run disambiguation SELECT: if no row for (token_id, user_id) → 404 'token not found'; if row has revoked_at set → 400 'token already revoked'.
Row is never deleted — UPDATE only sets revoked_at.

subtasks.title: Handle RevokePAT error cases and register all routes
details: On UPDATE DB error → APIError(c, 500, 'internal server error').
On follow-up SELECT DB error → APIError(c, 500, 'internal server error').

Implement RegisterRoutes(g *echo.Group) fully: g.POST('/user/tokens', h.CreatePAT), g.GET('/user/tokens', h.ListPATs), g.GET('/user/tokens/:token_id', h.GetPAT), g.DELETE('/user/tokens/:token_id', h.RevokePAT).
Remove any remaining panic('not implemented') stubs.

subtasks.title: Trace execution paths and verify no stubs remain
details: Verify 09-PATH-1 (create): POST /user/tokens → auth middleware → CreatePAT → generateTokenID → generateSecret → hashSecret → db.WithTx INSERT → HTTP 201.
